reason: Paper confirmed by both Semantic Scholar and Perplexity with matching title, authors, year, arXiv ID, and DOI. Additional evidence: NeurIPS 2024 acceptance, official proceedings DOI, GitHub repository, and citation count.
prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.** Its exact title is **Confidence Regulation Neurons in Language Models**. Authors are Alessandro Stolfo, Ben Wu (also listed as Ben Peng Wu), Wes Gurnee, Yonatan Belinkov, Xingyi Song, Mrinmaya Sachan, and Neel Nanda (with equal contribution noted for some).[1][2][4]

It was originally posted on **arXiv:2406.16254** (v1: 24 Jun 2024; v2: 8 Nov 2024).[2]

The paper was accepted and presented as a **NeurIPS 2024 poster** (Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 37, held 10-15 December 2024, Vancouver, Canada).[3][4][5] Published proceedings details include pages 125019-125049 and **DOI: 10.52202/079017-3970**.[5]

Additional resources: GitHub code repository at https://github.com/bpwu1/confidence-regulation-neurons.[7]
